data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-9228a484">
    <title>agent: implement StopCondition, valid-attempt retry budget, and repair-cycle escalation</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.
- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Move all budget/cost-cap decisions, including implementer plus adversarial reviewer group cost, into drain.stop(state).
- Relocate MinPower retry behavior from ddx-cfedee8e into the attempt/retry path without mutating requested harness/model/profile pins. A review_fixable_gap may raise MinPower for the repair attempt; spec gaps, missing acceptance, too-large, unsafe/out-of-scope, review errors beyond cap, intake ambiguity, and decomposition overflow block for the operator instead.
- Make no-progress budget count only valid implementation attempts where checkout/context existed and the implementer had an opportunity to modify or verify code. Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review errors, and operator-action states.
- Keep append-only cycle trace fields for every implementation cycle and repair cycle: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer slots, review result, final decision.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the adversarial review gate itself; that is tracked separately.
- Frontend display of stop reasons.
- Poll-interval defaults and idle workers; already handled by ddx-dc157075 / C10.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked, and drain.stop(state) is the only production budget/terminal stop classifier.
2. Cost-cap checks are removed from executor closure call sites and reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility is evaluated.
3. No-progress retry budget is incremented only for valid implementation attempts; TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human cases.
4. Review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK no longer reopens a closed bead. A review_fixable_gap prevents close, records review-fixable-gap, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers this.
5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.
6. MinPower retry/escalation behavior from ddx-cfedee8e is wired in the attempt/retry path without mutating explicit harness/model/profile pins. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins covers the path.
7. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for initial and repair cycles: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, and final decision. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers both cycles.
8. Downstream-consumer audit for cost-cap and stop-condition event names is committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
DEPENDENCY UPDATE 2026-05-06
Signal stop-condition work must use the Ctrl-C/SIGTERM graceful-shutdown contract in ddx-99a3be32. The old wording "signal received between attempts" is stale; FEAT-010 and SD-025 now require first signal to print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`, cancel the active context, classify in-flight work as mechanically disrupted, release non-terminal claims, and avoid no-progress cooldown.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, area:cost, kind:refactor, reliability, adr:024</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" base-rev="50a97bee8e02bb8d6f9cb4b1eaf70634c77e24ae" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Wrote the required no-changes rationale at [`.ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90/no_changes_rationale.txt`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-9228a484-20260507T001806-3762cb90/.ddx/executions/20260507T001806-3762cb90/no_changes_rationale.txt).

Reason: the bead is too broad for a single execution pass at the current decomposition depth cap, so I did not start a partial implementation or commit anything.
